justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named as 'mutually exclusive'. (2) All three agents' positions clearly attributed — Bird (100ms freshness rule), MJ (30s CDN TTL for scalability), Pippen (confirms CDN is hard operational requirement and flags the staleness violation). (3) Escalation to Coach K is explicit and precise. (4) No handoff brief produced — Magic explicitly refuses to produce one, stating it would cause Shaq to 'implement the wrong system'. (5) Four concrete, distinct resolution paths provided (relax consistency, relax scalability, tiered consistency model, push-based delivery), exceeding the minimum of 3.

observations.text: Opens with an unambiguous stop signal — 'Cannot produce handoff brief' — which is exactly the right behavior when constraints conflict
All three agents' positions are precisely attributed with specific numbers (100ms, 30s TTL, 50,000 concurrent viewers)
Provides 4 resolution paths instead of the required 3, each with clear ownership (who must decide/validate)
Correctly identifies that Pippen's note is a conflict indicator, not just an operational concern
Each resolution path names which agent must own the concession, showing understanding of the team's authority boundaries
Explicitly states the consequence of ignoring the conflict: 'Shaq will implement a system that satisfies one requirement while silently breaking the other'

agent_output_excerpt:
